title: leucas biflora var. procumbens
intro: Leucas biflora var. procumbens, also known as the procumbent leafy leucas or creeping leucas, is a type of flowering plant in the mint family that is native to tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Leucas biflora var. procumbens include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to alleviate digestive problems and reduce inflammation. Leucas biflora var. procumbens is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
